raw_consumer_statement:
AMEX contacted me on or about XXXX to verify two charges of {$2800.00} and {$2800.00}. I told them no, the charges were not mine nor had I ever ordered anything from the vendor they referenced ( I later determined this vendor sells gold and silver coins ). 

AMEX reversed one of the charges in my XXXX statement and the second in my XXXX statement. I thought that was the end of this. 

Then in XXXX AMEX informed me that they determined that I gave the vendor my card number and the charges were valid. They did this without any further information from me. I have called them many times and have been repeatedly told that this decision is final and to pay the bill. I HAVE REPEATEDLY TOLD THEM THESE ARE NOT MY CHARGES, AND IF THEY ARE PLEASE PRODUCE THE INFORMATION VERIFYING THAT I ORDERED AND RECIEVED WHATEVER MERCHANDISE THEY ALLEDGE I PURCHASED. I know they can not as this was a fraud! 

The charges now again appear on my bill in XX/XX/year>.
